title: Nuclear Decision-Making in Iran: A Rare Glimpse
summary: "On April 19, 2006, Iran's former secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, Hassan Rohani, publicly criticized his successor's handling of their country's nuclear negotiations with the West. In what was, for Iran, a rare public expression of discontent, he called for 'more balance in our decisions and [the need] to approach the issue with more reason and less emotion. . . .' "
body: Middle East Brief "On April 19, 2006, Iran's former secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, Hassan Rohani, publicly criticized his successor's handling of their country's nuclear negotiations with the West. In what was, for Iran, a rare public expression of discontent, he called for 'more balance in our decisions and [the need] to approach the issue with more reason and less emotion. . . .' " The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation Kane, Chen Zak. “Nuclear Decision-Making in Iran: A Rare Glimpse.” Crown Center for Middle East Studies, Brandeis University , May 2006 Copy to Clipboard Nuclear Decision-Making in Iran: A Rare Glimpse View Citation Print Want to read more?
